text: Authors must be responsible for what has been written in return for the credit and recognition that authorship brings.
source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

text: For any multi-authored article based on a student thesis, research study or dissertation, the student should normally be the first author, unless there is documented evidence that the journal required that the authorship should be written in a different order.
source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

text: However, the Chair of the student’s Program Committee should serve as the corresponding author with any journal.
source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

text: ACADEMIC MISCONDUCT All members of the AIT community are expected to comply with the relevant Institute policy and procedures in the conduct of research and to observe high standards of academic integrity and ethical behavior in publishing research outputs by self- regulation and by adherence to professional standards.
source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

text: DISCIPLINARY ACTION Any member of the AIT community who is aware of academic misconduct in research or publication may report the incident or practice to the School Dean or directly to the Vice President for Academic Affairs.
source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

text: The procedures for disciplinary actions shall be according to the Institute Policy and Procedures on Employment of Faculty and of Staff:
source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

text: Student Handbook (section on Student Code of Conduct) Keywords Rationale for Revisions: • (18 Mar 2022) Administrative restructuring and minor edits • (7 Feb 2011) For articles where the student is the sole author, the student serves as the corresponding author with any journal. • (19 Nov 2010) For a multi-authored article, the Chair of the student’s Program Committee should serve as the corresponding author with any journal;
source_document: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

text: For self-support students and holders of externally-managed scholarships (externally-supported/sponsored students who have their own funds): (1) With regard to self-paying students and students with external sponsors, first semester/term fees must be paid in advance and/or fully paid upon enrolment, otherwise they will not be allowed to register.

Consequently, the student ID and Internet/e-mail access will not be released by Registry if fees are not paid. (2) Continuing full-time postgraduate (PG) students (Master, Doctor and Diploma), i.e., those who completed at least one semester/term, will be invoiced for the applicable registration, tuition, and other fees each semester/term from the second semester/term onwards, which must be paid in advance and/or fully paid before each semester/term starts, otherwise they will not be allowed to register. (3) For students under the Professional Master program, they will be invoiced for the applicable registration, tuition, and other fees for the second semester/term.
The invoice must be paid in advance and/or fully paid up before each semester/term starts, otherwise they will not be allowed to register.

The invoice must be paid in advance and/or fully paid up before each semester/term starts, otherwise they will not be allowed to register. (5) Continuing part-time, including flexible program, students will be invoiced for the applicable registration, tuition, and other fees each semester/term from the second semester/term onwards, which must be paid in advance and/or fully paid before each semester/term starts, otherwise they will not be allowed to register.

Employee-students follow the stipulations on Payment of Fees in the P&P on Employee- Students on Part-Time Status (AA-8-8-2 IV.) (6) Registrations for each semester/term can only be activated upon full payment of tuition and all other fees. (7) If tuition and other fees remain unpaid by the start of a semester/term, a student’s status shall be ‘suspended due to financial inability’.

Thus, they cannot enroll in any course, nor can they avail of student privileges and benefits during the suspended period such as use of an AIT ID card, access to SIS and registration records, staying in AIT accommodation, use of Library and School facilities, discount of children in the AIT International School (AITIS), etc.
Their educational visas may also be cancelled.

Suspension status can only be lifted upon full payment of the outstanding fees and applicable interest charges before the deadline for adding/dropping of courses. (8) Late payments, i.e., made after the start of the semester/term, but before the deadline for adding/dropping of courses, will be subject to an interest penalty of 1.5% per month. (9) Online registration will no longer be allowed after the deadline for adding/dropping of courses. (10) Additional payments for audited and repeated courses, as well as beyond the minimum 3 credits for part-time students, registered for the semester/term must be fully paid one week before the mid- semester/term examination period starts. (11) If additional payments for audited and repeated courses remain unpaid a week prior to the mid-semester/term examination period, students with outstanding fees will be removed from the roster, and they will not be allowed to take any examinations.

CREDIT POLICY (12) If fees remain unpaid after the deadline for adding/dropping of courses of the semester/term in which a student was ‘suspended due to financial inability’, the student shall be recommended for dismissal.

Once dismissed, students who are staying in campus accommodation shall be asked to vacate their dormitory rooms; and their educational visas will be cancelled. (14) ‘Dismissed’ students can no longer avail of student privileges and benefits after their dismissal.

Their student status can only be reinstated upon full payment of the applicable registration, tuition and other fees for the following semester/term, which must be paid in advance and/or fully paid before the semester/term starts. (15) Students can be on ‘dismissed’ status for a maximum of two (2) semester or three (3) terms or one calendar year only.

Upon readmission, the possibility to transfer all courses taken before will be governed by the policy of having taken the courses not more than five (5) years ago. (16) The student cannot avail of the facility of non-payment one more time during the reinstated or re-admitted period.

Permanent dismissal will be applied to those who will be dismissed again due to non-payment of fees on time. (17) Graduating students without financial clearance will not be allowed to take the final defense of their dissertation/thesis/research study/project/ internship. (18) Graduating students with unpaid fees will not be permitted to graduate nor will they be allowed to rent gowns, and join the graduation rehearsal and ceremony.

An interest penalty of 1.5% per month will apply on outstanding fees of graduating students. (19) Provisional transcripts are not provided to self-support and externally- supported/sponsored students with any outstanding registration, tuition, and all other fees. (20) Overdue accounts shall be reviewed periodically, and follow-up collections shall be initiated.
Sponsors must be invoiced for outstanding dues and requested to send AIT a promissory note detailing when and how payment will be made.

The Registry will provide the Transcript or Degree Certificate to the concerned Office to be forwarded to the external donor. (6) The Office of Student Affairs (OSA) shall regularly follow up with external donor partners to ensure that payments are disbursed.
Writing-off of doubtful accounts will be requested by OFIN and have to be approved by the President, or their VP designate.

Student Accommodation Rental (1) Students are required to pay a security deposit, which is collected in advance. (2) Housing rental is payable on the first day of each month. (3) With regard to unpaid rental and utilities, interest of 1.5% per month is imposed if the amount remains unpaid after 30 days. (4) If the outstanding amount exceeds two-month rental, the students will be required to vacate the rooms. (5) Eviction letters will be sent by OFAM/Accommodation Unit to the concerned students with a grace period to pay the outstanding amount, which include interest.

Otherwise, concerned students have to vacate the room within two weeks after the given deadline to pay. (6) If accommodation and other fees remain unpaid beyond two months, a student’s status shall be ‘suspended/dismissed due to financial inability’.

Employee Accommodation Rental (1) Employees are required to pay a two-month deposit, which is collected in advance. (2) Invoices will be billed monthly and payment will be made through deduction from the employees’ monthly payroll account. (3) In the case an employee has an insufficient amount available for deduction through the payroll account, an interest penalty of 1.5% per month will be imposed on the balance that remains unpaid. (4) If a tenant defaults on any payment for a period of two successive months, the housing lease agreement may be terminated by written Page 5/9
CREDIT POLICY notice from the Accommodation Unit, and the tenant is required to vacate the housing unit within two weeks.
Writing-off of outstanding invoices, including interest due, will be requested by the OFAM Director and have to be approved by the President, or their VP designate, and forwarded to OFIN for action.
All Requests for Travel Authorization (RTA) and Promissory Note (PN) by employees are required to be settled by the indicated due dates.

CREDIT POLICY AIT students of part-time degree programs and on extension are not entitled to discounts on AITIS fees for their children. (3) Where both parents are entitled to receive assistance (discount/subsidy) with fees for the education of dependent children, the lower discount / subsidy rate applies. (4) Where one parent is a seconded employee to AIT, concessionaire on the AIT campus, or are non-AIT employees, they are not eligible for any discount/subsidy of fees for the education of their children studying at AITIS.

With regard to parents (AIT students and employees) with more than one child: (1) Fees for the first child shall be paid in full within 30 days from the first day of the semester. (1) Fees for the rest of the children shall be paid in full by the end of the second month of the semester.
With regard to other students, fees shall be paid in full within 30 days from the first day of the semester.
If the payment is not received within the periods stipulated, the AITIS students concerned shall be removed from the rolls of the school and shall not be allowed to attend classes.
Lessees are required to pay a three-month advance deposit to take on the lease of premises.
Dining (1) Personal meals are required to be settled.
No credit shall be granted. (2) Meals that are charged to non-personal accounts must be authorized and payment is to be settled through internal charge.
Invoices are required to be settled by the indicated due date.
Overdue accounts shall be reviewed periodically and follow-up on collections shall be initiated.
Any concerns involving student welfare that is related to accommodation should be brought to the attention of the Director of Student Affairs.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: For subsequent semesters, these students may opt to reside off-campus.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Married Students are permitted to bring their immediate families (spouse and children) to join them during their studies.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Families of non-resident students may not reside on campus if the non-resident student is off-campus.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Master’s or doctoral students with spouses who are AIT employees must register for staff accommodation.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students should move with the spouse when staff accommodation becomes available.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students, who will study in another country as part of the Exchange/Dual Degree program, are required to vacate their rooms while they study abroad.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Exceptions to this may only be given to those whose exchange program is less than two months.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: All guests must be registered with the Accommodation Unit three days before arrival.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: With prior approval of the Accommodation Unit, guests may stay in student dormitories for a maximum period of one week per semester.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: In the case of overstaying guests, special prior permission must be obtained from the OFAM Director and a daily rent will be charged for the room.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: The Institute shall in no way be held responsible for the legal status of guests.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Visas, residence permits, etc. shall be the responsibility of the guests.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students may put their names on the waiting list and live in employee accommodation subject to availability.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students are allowed to temporarily stay in such units until such time that they have been allocated a room in student accommodation or until advised by the Accommodation Unit.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students are required to vacate their rooms within five days (including weekends) after their graduation/completion of studies.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: It is recommended that new students be on campus during the arrival period specified in the admission offer letter. ● New students are served in a queue based on a ‘take a number’ system drawn by lot (i.e., students stand in line and in this order select a random number from the box; this number becomes the queuing number and, in this sequence, students wait their turn to select an accommodation unit). ● New students may choose from all the available units when their queuing number is called.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: New and continuing students wishing to move into another category of rooms may queue during the period designated by the Accommodation Unit.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Queuing requires presentation of a student ID. ● A student may queue for a maximum of three dorm categories in order of preference. ● By queuing in more than one category the student risks being allocated the least preferred room. ● A student may put special requirements in the "specifications" column of the waiting list. ● A student residing on campus must put their name on the waiting list in person, at the Accommodation Unit.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: CAMPUS ACCOMMODATION FOR STUDENTS ● Only continuing students, who are off-campus on data collection or on approved leave, may put their names on the waiting list through email to accommodation@ait.asia.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: They must provide the approximate date of their arrival on campus. ● A student wishing to move within the same dorm category requires prior approval of the OFAM Director.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Allocation According to Waiting List ● An offer for transfer/relocation from one unit to another according to the waiting list shall start during the period designated by the Accommodation Unit and is done every Friday. ● Allocation based on the waiting list is done only during the semester.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: The Accommodation Unit will inform concerned students on unit allocation specifics (e.g. new room number, dates of transfer, etc.) by email. ● A student shall have five working days to accept the offer.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Failure to accept the allocated unit shall result in the removal of the student from the waiting list. ● The allocation to continuing students on the waiting list will be suspended during the arrival of new students, up until the last day of adding/dropping. ● Category 2 dorms (E, J, K) are allocated to continuing students only.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: New and continuing students may apply for student villages following the policies below:
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Unmarried Students in Shared Unit ● Students may apply for a shared unit provided they are able to find a group of three students with similar entry and graduation dates, who mutually agree to live together.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: A change in the grouping after allocation is not permitted unless otherwise approved by the OFAM Director. ● If shared units become available, the first group on the waiting list will be notified.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: The students concerned will be given five working days to confirm the offer to move; otherwise, the group may lose the offer.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: All maintenance queries must be reported to the Call Center on 02 524 5800 between 0800 and 1700 hours, Monday to Saturday.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Regular cleaning and hygiene of the unit has to be maintained by the student.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: OFAM is authorized to undertake random inspection and may lay sanctions if the unit is found to be dirty and unhygienic.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students have to upkeep the cleanliness of the common area and landscape around the building.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Cooking is not permitted, except in accommodation units where cooking facilities or shared cooking facilities are provided.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students are allowed to install outdoor antennas or cable TV, only with the prior agreement of the Accommodation Unit.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students are not allowed to install heavy appliances, such as air-conditioners, in accommodation units that are not designed for such facilities, i.e. in Student Villages and Category 1 dorms.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Upon leaving the accommodation unit, students are required to leave their units clean, remove all possessions, and to leave the unit and its furniture and equipment in the same condition as when the tenancy began.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students shall not disturb fellow students in the residential areas.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Games and activities shall cease at 2300 hours at all sports fields and courts in and near campus residential areas.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Noisy group studies and parties are not allowed in student accommodation.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Alternative rooms designated for such purpose must be used.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students who misplace their accommodation unit keys may ask the Accommodation Unit to open their dorms.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Furniture and appliances should not be removed from the accommodation unit.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Repair and replacement of furniture and appliances must be requested from and Page 6/8
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: CAMPUS ACCOMMODATION FOR STUDENTS approved by the Accommodation Unit, which may ask the tenant to pay additional charges, in situations where damage to furniture and appliances has resulted from direct negligence by the tenant.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students who cook in dormitories where cooking is prohibited may face a minimum fine of Baht 2,000, or the actual costs of the damage caused by cooking.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students who install heavy appliances, such as air-conditioners, in accommodation units not designed for such facilities, will be fined Baht 2,000 and charged for the services related to the removal of the appliances.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students found guilty may face a fine of Baht 2,000, or the actual costs of the damage caused by smoking or burning.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Pets are not allowed in student accommodation.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students disturbing the peace of their surroundings shall receive a formal reprimand as a first warning.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Repeated disturbances may face further sanctions.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Rooms must be used for residential purposes only.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students who do not maintain the upkeep and cleanliness of their bedroom, bathroom, corridor, balcony, and cooking facilities, where provided, will be fined Baht 2,000 and charged for cleaning services of the unit.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Students who do not upkeep the standard conditions of the common area and landscape around the building, particularly with reference to cluttering the common areas/corridors, and planting plants, will be fined Baht 2,000 and charged for the cleaning and removal services.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Self-funded students are required to pay a one-month advance deposit.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: Special and exchange students are required to pay the rent in full for the whole duration of their stay on campus.
source_document: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

text: The accomplishments, activities and individual differences of other people must be respected.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: Members of the Institute’s community must not abuse the services, benefits, opportunities and facilities of the Institute, nor use them for their personal financial gain.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: AIT employees, students and residents must act in the interests of the Institute and avoid engaging in any actions contrary to the policies and interests of the Institute.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: AIT employees in supervisory positions must at all times use their authority ethically, with respect and sensitivity, and in accordance with the Institute’s policies.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: An employee must disclose to their supervisor any actual or potential conflict of interest with the interests of the Institute, which may arise from outside activities, professional or otherwise.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: When expressing personal opinions outside the Institute, AIT employees and students are required to note explicitly that the opinions expressed are personal and do not necessarily represent the position of the Institute.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: Acceptance by an employee of a gift valued at THB 3,000 or more must be reported by the employee to their Unit Head within 15 days of acceptance, giving details as to the description of the item, sources and circumstance leading to the acceptance.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: No employee or student shall deny or grant a right or a privilege to another employee or student wholly or partly on the basis of nationality, race, social background, gender, age, marital status, family status, sexual orientation, or disability.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: An employee or student adjudged to have violated the Institute’s policy on harassment, abuse or discrimination will be subject to disciplinary action.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: A Grievance Committee shall receive all grievances.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: The Grievance Committee shall elect a Chair from among its members, which will have a term of two years and can serve for not more than two consecutive terms.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: The Grievance Committee shall perform three main roles: a.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: The Grievance Committee must be prepared to identify breaches of the Institute P&Ps, etc.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: In performing their duties, they must protect the interests of all members of the AIT community. c.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: The Grievance Committee should express in writing what came to light during the processing of grievances.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: They must inform the President of all settlements made during the grievance process.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: A complainant is required to initiate the grievance process by submitting the grievance in writing (please refer to the Grievance / Complaint Fact Sheet in the Annex as a guide) to the Grievance Committee or discussing their grievance in person with any member of the Grievance Committee. b.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: When there are strong grounds to conclude that a serious crime such as rape, grievous bodily harm, assault, or theft has been committed, the Grievance Committee should consult with the President, and if necessary, inform the police.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: All counseling is off the record, and in confidence. d. ‘Direct confrontation’ involves the complainant writing a statement to the individual(s) concerned, describing the unwelcome behavior and its effect, and stating that the behavior must cease. e.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: The complainant may seek the advice of the Grievance Committee on what to say or write. f.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: The person(s) towards whom the direct confrontation is directed may likewise use consultation and direct confrontation in their response. g.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: Both the complainant and complainee(s) are permitted to request one observer each, to monitor all proceedings. k.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: Outcomes of the mediation shall include explicit written agreements to be submitted to the Grievance Committee. m.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: If the informal grievance process, fails to lead to a satisfactory resolution of the complaint, the complainant who has suffered from harassment, abuse or discrimination may lodge a formal complaint with the Grievance Committee.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: The formal complaint must be in writing, and should state clearly all facts for consideration. c.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: The grievance must include: i. the alleged violation and/or misapplication of a stated policy or procedure; ii. a statement of the relevant facts supporting the complainant’s position, e.g., date(s), event(s), name(s)/title of person(s) involved, witnesses; and iii. the remedy sought by the complainant d.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: The Grievance Committee is required to address the complaint in utmost confidentiality and with due regard to the privacy of the complainant, and of the person accused (‘the defendant’). e.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: When the Grievance Committee is involved in a grievance procedure, they should keep in mind that facts are the substance of any grievance, and the fact-gathering process is of fundamental importance.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: In gathering the facts, the Grievance Committee should put all points in writing even if the grievance is not presented.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: In obtaining evidence, the Grievance Committee should concentrate on recording facts, not arguments.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: Having gathered the relevant facts, the Grievance Committee should proceed to analyze the grievance.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: When further action is required, the President shall appoint a Grievance Tribunal. b.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: The written notification of the hearing should give: the time and place of the hearing; ● the name(s) of the complainant(s); ● Page 8/11
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: The Grievance Tribunal shall be convened at the earliest possible date after the formal complaint.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: Hearings shall be open only to the members of the Grievance Tribunal, the complainant(s) and the defendant, and any witnesses called. b.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: The Grievance Tribunal must seek to ascertain, to the best of its ability, the facts of the case. c.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: In ascertaining the facts of the case, the Tribunal may call witnesses to testify before the Grievance Tribunal.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: After having received the report of the Grievance Tribunal, the President may take further legal advice, before acting. g.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: The President may delegate their responsibilities to a Vice President.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: When the defendant is an employee, pending the report of the Grievance Tribunal, the President may, if they consider such action justified, suspend the defendant from work until completion of the investigation. b.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: A copy of the report shall be kept in the Office of the President, and be accessible to the immediate Supervisor, Director of OHRS, Director of OSA, or the Director of OAA.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: The Grievance Tribunal will recommend whether a copy of the report should be placed in the defendant’s file in OHRS or OSA.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: If either the complainant or the defendant is not satisfied with the decision of the President, they may appeal to the Board, through its Audit Committee.
source_document: PA-2-1-2 Ethical Behavior and Grievance Process AMT8Dec2021.pdf

text: If make-up classes are scheduled out of regular class hours, the re-scheduled classes should not clash with the schedules of students in the class.
Students have the right to expect that their grade in a course should be determined only by academic achievement consistent with the aims and content of that course.

At the beginning of the course, the instructor should make known the criteria that will be considered in determining the grade such as class attendance, class participation, papers, examinations, projects and presentations.
The student will be notified, and allowed to appeal.
All students have the right to a peaceful, healthy learning environment in which free discussion is encouraged in the common interest of the pursuit of knowledge.
They are expected to arrive by the beginning of the class, and remain for the class period.
Students must use the AIT Library and all educational resources of the Institute in a responsible manner.
Students may not deface or vandalize books, periodicals, and/or computer resources in the AIT Library as well as computer lab hardware, software and related equipment.
Students must abide by the Institute’s acceptable use policy for IT resources.

While students have the right to freedom of expression, including the right to dissent, protest, or take reasoned exception to the information and views offered in any course, this expression must not interfere with the rights of others, impede the progress of instruction, or disrupt the processes of the Institute.
STUDENT CODE OF CONDUCT Students at AIT are expected to meet the highest standards of personal, ethical and moral conduct.

If there is suspicion of cheating during an examination, in an assignment or a research project, the instructor/invigilator should immediately call the attention of the student and put a notation on segments of the exam, assignment or project where cheating has been suspiciously committed.

The instructor/invigilator should seek an explanation from the student at the end of the examination or when the assignment or project was submitted, and submit an incident report to the Department Head, who should then notify the School Dean.
Notes of the interview will be recorded and should be agreed upon by all parties.
If the interview panel is satisfied that there is no evidence of cheating, the Department Head and the instructor/invigilator may recommend to the School Dean to give no penalty.

However, if cheating was proven, the Department Head and the instructor/invigilator may recommend to the School Dean to disregard the original examination score and require the student to submit an equivalent piece of work or retake a different version of the examination.

Upon receipt of the retake, the School Dean may decide to: ● Downgrade by 1 step the grade the student will receive upon repetition of the exam or resubmission of the assignment/project; ● Give a score of ‘zero’ for the specific exam, project or assignment; or ● Give a failing grade or “F” for the course; or ● Recommend the student for suspension or dismissal.
The appeal should be addressed to the School Dean.

If School Dean feels that there is evidence of serious dishonesty and the recommendation of the Field of Study Coordination and instructor/invigilator was suspension or dismissal, the matter should be elevated to the Vice President for Academic Affairs.

committee consisting of members from outside the School where the student belongs. ● The investigating committee should consider all the available evidence, including that of the incident report submitted by the School, and the student accused.
This report should be submitted promptly, if possible permitting the required action to be taken before the beginning of the next semester.

The Vice President for Academic Affairs may: ● No penalty; or ● Downgrade by 1 step the grade the student will receive upon repetition of the exam or resubmission of the assignment/project; ● Give a score of ‘zero’ for the specific exam, project or assignment; or ● Give a failing grade or “F” for the course; or ● Suspend the student from the Institute for a specific period; or ● Recommend dismissal especially if there is evidence that the incident is already the second offence, a report to the Academic Senate should be made for decision.
The appeal should be addressed to the Vice President for Academic Affairs.

Plagiarism and Failure to Acknowledge Sources All members of the AIT community are expected to comply with the relevant Institute policy and procedures in the conduct of research and to observe high standards of academic integrity and ethical behavior in publishing research outputs by self-regulation and by adherence to professional standards.
A complaint against a student should be made to the Director, OSA.

If the matter cannot be settled informally the complaint must be confirmed in writing and be presented by the complainant to the Director, OSA, who will decide whether or not to proceed with an investigation.

If an investigation is authorized, a meeting of the Standing Disciplinary Commission will be convened thru the Vice President for Academic Affairs (who is also the Chair), which constitutes the following membership: ● Secretary of the Commission (to be appointed by the VPAA) ● Director, Office of Student Affairs (OSA) Additional members for cases involving students may consist of: ● Dean concerned ● Department Head concerned ● Student Advisor ● Coordinator, Student Welfare Unit ● Student Union President b.
The student against whom the complaint has been made shall have the right to be present while the Commission hears the evidence against him or her.
He/She may question witnesses. d.
Otherwise, he/she may face: ● suspension for a stated period;
Arrival on Campus New students are expected to arrive within a period of 3 days before the first day of the semester.
All students are required to bring one passport size photo (4 cm x 6 cm) to be attached to the registration form.
For non-Thai students, four additional photos must be submitted to the Government Relations Unit for visa.
All new students are required to attend the orientation seminar.
Writing Placement Test for Registration/Enrolment All degree program students (including CAS and Certificate leading to Master) must take the AIT Writing Test (AIT-WT) upon arrival on campus.
Students will only be allowed to enroll for courses after they have taken the AIT-WT.
You may find it necessary to supplement this allowance from your own funds.
A minimum fee deposit of 500 Baht is required to open a bank account and another 300 Baht for ATM card at the local bank near campus.

Students on External / Self-Support or AIT Fellowships As you have agreed to the conditions of the offer of admission, you are expected to have understood the stipulations in the offer as Self-Support, External-Support or AIT Fellowship holder.
These conditions are not negotiable and cannot be changed after enrollment.

Students within the normal study period must pay the semester fee of Baht 188,000 for 12 credits Tuition Fee and Registration Fee in advance and/or fully paid up upon enrolment but not later than the deadline for adding/dropping of courses, otherwise they will not be allowed to register.
Late payments, i.e., made after the start of the semester, but before the deadline for adding/dropping of courses, will be subject to an interest penalty of 1.5% per month.
This has to be fully paid one week before mid-semester examination period starts.
Otherwise, student will be removed from the roster and will not be allowed to sit in examinations.
Students must submit a leave form if they are travelling out of the country.

Baht 1,250 is the initial premium for new students of August Intake, to be renewed in January semester of the following Medical year; for students studying in January and August semesters, Insurance the annual premium is Baht 2,500; for students graduating in May or during the inter-semester, the final premium is Baht 1,250 for standard insurance plan.
This would have to be approved by the Program Committee Chair and the Department Head.

Final research expenses must be accounted for in the Student Research Expenses
The settlement should be supported by collated receipts issued under the name of Asian Institute of Technology.
Total collated receipts and expenses with no receipts (as reported in the no receipts form) must be equivalent to total settlement amount.
Students who are allowed to extend beyond the normal study period have the following privileges:
Before a student is accepted for enrolment, the Admissions and Scholarships Unit (ASU) is required to ensure that there is proof of financial support for the student’s full study program.
For self-paying students, the proof can be in the form of bank statement, cash, etc. and for external/sponsored students, authorized documentary proof of sponsorship must be submitted.

With regard to self-paying students and students with external sponsors, first semester fees must be paid in advance and/or fully paid up upon enrolment, otherwise they will not be allowed to register.

Consequently, the student ID and Internet/e-mail access will not be released by Registry if fees are not paid. (ii) Continuing full-time students, i.e. those who completed at least one semester, will be invoiced for registration fee and 12-credits each semester from the second semester onwards, which must be paid in advance and/or fully paid up before each semester starts, otherwise they will not be allowed to register. (iii) Continuing part-time students, e.g. employee-students, will be invoiced for registration fee and 3-credits each semester from the second semester onwards, which must be paid in advance and/or fully paid up before each semester starts, otherwise they will not be allowed to register. (iv) Registrations for each semester can only be activated upon full payment of tuition and other fees.
If fees remain unpaid by the start of a semester, a student’s status shall be ‘suspended due to financial inability’.
Registration for the current semester and viewing of grades and transcripts will be disallowed. (vi) ‘Suspended’ students lose their status as students, and are not charged registration fee.

Thus, they cannot enroll in any course nor can they avail of student privileges and benefits during the suspended period such as use of AIT ID card, access to SIS and registration record, staying in AIT accommodation, use of Library and School facilities, discount of children in AITIS, etc.
Their educational visas may also be cancelled.

Additional payments for audited and repeated courses, as well as beyond the minimum 3 credits for part- time students, registered for the semester must be fully paid one week before mid-semester examination period starts. (viii) If additional payments for audited and repeated courses remain unpaid a week prior to the mid-semester examination period, students with outstanding fees will be removed from the roster, and they will not be allowed to sit in examinations. (ix) If additional payments for audited and repeated courses remain unpaid a week prior to the mid-semester examination period, students with outstanding fees will be removed from the roster, and they will not be allowed to sit in examinations.
If fees remain unpaid after the deadline for adding/dropping of courses of the semester in which a student was ‘suspended due to financial inability’, the student shall be recommended for dismissal.

Once dismissed, students who are staying on campus accommodation shall be asked to vacate their dormitory rooms; and their educational visas will be cancelled. (xii) ‘Dismissed’ students can no longer avail of student privileges and benefits after their dismissal.

Their student status can only be reinstated upon full payment of the registration fee and 12-credits for the following semester, which must be paid in advance and/or fully paid up before the semester starts. (xiii) Graduating students without financial clearance will not be allowed to have the final defense of their dissertation/thesis/research study/project/ internship. (xiv) Graduating students with unpaid fees will not be permitted to graduate nor will they be allowed to rent gowns, and join the graduation rehearsal and ceremony.

Interest penalty of 1.5% per month will apply on outstanding fees of graduating students. (vi) Provisional transcripts are not provided to self-support and externally supported/sponsored students with outstanding fees. (vii) Overdue accounts shall be reviewed periodically and follow-up on collections shall be initiated.

With regard to students who received AIT-administered scholarships and those who are covered by active MoU/MoA/LoA with AIT, credit is extended automatically. (ii) For these students, interest penalty for delayed payments will not be applied. (iii) Students with donor funding who resign after the deadline for adding and dropping of courses will be liable to repay the total amount of tuition and registration fees that have been charged to the donor. (iv) Graduating students who are not able to complete financial clearance before graduation, due to outstanding financial obligations caused by delay in the transmission of payment by their donors are allowed to graduate and join the graduation ceremony if there is an endorsement from the Director of External Relations Office (OEXR) based on the confirmation from the donor that payment will be transmitted to AIT soon.

The Registry will provide the Transcript or Degree Certificate to ERCO for sending to the external donor. (vi) The ASU shall regularly follow up with the external donor partners to ensure that payments are disbursed.
Writing-off of outstanding student bills including interest due will be requested by the concerned Dean and have to be approved by the AIT Management and forwarded to Finance for action.

Writing-off of outstanding bills including interest due will be requested by Head- OFAM and have to be approved by the AIT Management and forwarded to Finance for action.
All Request for Travel Authorization (RTA) and Promissory Note (PN) to faculty/staff are required to be settled by the indicated due dates.

(i) Fees for the first child shall be paid up in full within 30 days from the first day of the semester. (ii) Fees for the second child shall be paid up in full by the end of the second month of the semester.

(ii) Administrative restructuring; to reduce arrears) =============================================================== New Online Payment Options for Students (as of 29 April 2021) As part of Finance Department customer service enhancements and with the current COVID-19 situation, starting from 1st May 2021, students will be provided with several online payment options for paying any invoice (Tuition & Fee, Accommodation, etc.).
In order to make use of this feature, students must login to SIS/SOMSIS, and click the menu Invoice.
From the invoice list shown, students should select the invoice they want to pay and click the button "Pay Online".
Students must have an account at any Thai bank and its mobile app installed in their phone.
Credit card (Visa/Master/JCB) Students must have a valid Visa/Master/JCB card.
WeChat/Alipay Students must have a valid WeChat/Alipay wallet and its app must be installed on the phone.

Student Welfare Matters ● Gender related matters ● Personal Counseling/Wellness Counseling by a professional psychologist ● Dispute Mediation ● Any other problem that a student may wish to seek advise

Medical Insurance/AIT Student Health Benefits Program All new AIT degree program students (except those under EMBA-BKK and Special Program students) are required to take compulsory standard medical insurance plan from Generali Life Assurance (Thailand) Co.

Please be informed that due to the COVID-19 pandemic, you may check your member status, claim status, benefits and channels to contact the Generali Insurance Call Center via mobile application (E card).

If you have not yet filled up the Generali application form (in attachment), please kindly complete it and return the form to Email : student-welfare@ait.ac.th Any student who may want upgraded health benefits or enrol their dependents (spouse and children) may avail of the other options provided by the company as described in their manual.
Donor and externally-funded students may consult the Admissions and Scholarships Unit if their sponsors have provisions for such.
Health Declaration (Insured’s Application Form) of each eligible member will be required and has to be approved by the insurer prior to the effective date of insurance.
Exclusions of Generali Health Insurance Benefit No benefit shall be payable under any of the following occurrences:
All dependents have to complete the health declaration form and get approval from Generali before the effective date.
Addition of dependents within the valid period of insurance is not allowed by Generali.
